rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical oxidative sulfenocyclization of unsaturated carboxylic acids with diaryl disulfides to give thio-substituted lactones
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of diphenyl disulfide (PhSSPh) to generate a thiyl radical (PhS•) and a sulfenyl cation (PhS+); cathode reduces protons to H2

key_intermediates.name: thiiranium/sulfonium adduct of alkene with carboxylic acid (C)

electron_flow: PhSSPh is preferentially oxidized at the anode (CV: oxidation peak of 2a at 1.86 V, lower than 1a at 2.37 V) to give PhS• and PhS+; the electrophilic PhS+ adds to the alkene of the unsaturated carboxylic acid to form a sulfonium intermediate, which undergoes intramolecular nucleophilic attack by the carboxylate followed by deprotonation to give the lactone, while protons are reduced to H2 at the cathode.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction inhibited; product yield reduced to 30%, indicating a radical intermediate

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction inhibited; product yield reduced to 27%, consistent with radical pathway

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of 1a, 2a, and mixture
probe_reagents.observation: Oxidation peak of 2a at 1.86 V vs Ag/AgCl, lower than 1a (2.37 V), showing disulfide is preferentially oxidized at anode

notes: The mechanism is proposed as a radical/cationic crossover initiated by anodic oxidation of the disulfide rather than the alkene-COOH substrate, supported by CV. Method extends to δ-lactones (from δ,ε-unsaturated acids) and tellurated γ-lactones using PhTeTePh.
